build.//: Uses `next build --webpack` for the same reason as `serve`: Next 16 defaults to Turbopack, which refuses the private-plugins symlink (points outside the repo root) and the ESM plugin packages, failing to resolve @veritas/plugin-magi. NODE_ENV=production is forced because nx auto-loads the root .env (which pins NODE_ENV=development for dev); without this the production prerender of /_global-error fails with a non-standard NODE_ENV. Output stays in apps/veritas-client/.next (run with `next start`).

serve.//: Uses `next dev --webpack` because Next 16 defaults to Turbopack, which refuses the private-plugins symlink (points outside the repo root) and the ESM plugin packages. The @nx/next:server `turbo:false` option does not force webpack under Next 16.

options.command: env -u NO_COLOR pnpm exec jest --config apps/veritas-client/jest.config.ts --passWithNoTests --watchman=false
